name: Phi Tai Hong
description: The Phi Tai Hong is a vengeful ghost in Thai folklore, specifically the spirit of a person who died a violent and unjust death, often through murder, accident, or suicide. These spirits are said to be filled with intense anger and resentment, driven by a thirst for revenge against those who caused their demise or any living being they encounter. The Phi Tai Hong is considered one of the most dangerous and malevolent ghosts in Thai belief, capable of causing sickness, misfortune, and even death to those who cross their path. They are believed to linger near the site of their death or the location where their body was found, forever reliving their final moments and seeking retribution. Offerings and rituals are often performed to appease these spirits and prevent them from causing harm. Their appearance can vary, but they are generally depicted as terrifying and decaying figures, reflecting the violent nature of their death. They are often associated with feelings of dread and fear, embodying the unresolved pain and suffering of the deceased.
subtitle: Vengeful Spirit of Violent Death
appearance: The Phi Tai Hong's appearance is often described as grotesque and horrifying, reflecting the violent nature of their death. They can appear as a decaying corpse, covered in blood and wounds. Their features are often distorted with rage and pain, and their eyes burn with a malevolent intensity. Some accounts describe them as having a shadowy or translucent form, while others depict them as solid and tangible. They may also exhibit signs of the specific trauma that caused their death, such as broken bones or stab wounds. Overall, their appearance is designed to inspire fear and dread in those who encounter them.
habitat: Phi Tai Hong are typically found haunting the location of their death or the place where their body was discovered. This could be a roadside where they were killed in an accident, a house where they were murdered, or a forest where they committed suicide. They are also believed to linger near places associated with their past life, such as their former home or workplace. These spirits are often bound to these locations, unable to move on until their anger and resentment are resolved or they are properly appeased.
behavior: The Phi Tai Hong is driven by a relentless desire for revenge. They are known to haunt and torment those they believe are responsible for their death, or even innocent bystanders who happen to cross their path. Their behavior can range from subtle acts of manipulation and misfortune to violent attacks and possession. They are often depicted as malevolent and unpredictable, capable of inflicting great harm on the living. They may also attempt to lure others to their death, seeking to recreate the circumstances of their own demise. The ultimate goal of the Phi Tai Hong is to find peace, but their overwhelming rage and resentment often prevent them from achieving it.

keyMyths.title: The Murdered Wife
keyMyths.description: A woman murdered by her jealous husband becomes a Phi Tai Hong, haunting him until he confesses his crime and seeks redemption through Buddhist practices. Her spirit finds peace after his repentance.

sources.author: Ruangdet Panthep
sources.description: A comprehensive guide to various types of Thai ghosts, including the Phi Tai Hong.
sources.text: A comprehensive guide to various types of Thai ghosts, including the Phi Tai Hong.
